title: Intermittent Packet Loss — Core Datacenter Switch

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] 15-40% packet loss between DC-CORE-SW-01 and distribution layer
servicenow_seed.description: Network monitoring (SolarWinds) has alerted on 15-40% packet loss between DC-CORE-SW-01 (Cisco Nexus 9K) and the distribution-layer switches. VoIP calls are dropping and VDI sessions are disconnecting intermittently across all tenants. The link has been degraded for 22 minutes. No maintenance window is active.

correct_steps: Acknowledge the SolarWinds alert and validate with the reporting caller
Review SNMP trap logs and interface error counters on DC-CORE-SW-01
Set Impact 1 / Urgency 1 — all tenants and VoIP affected
Check for related VoIP, VDI, and application tickets — link as children
Escalate to Network Operations (NOC) for immediate switch investigation
Do not reboot the core switch without NOC lead approval — risk of full outage
Open a major incident record and notify IT management and comms team
rationale: Core datacenter switch degradation is a Tier 3 / NOC incident. 15-40% packet loss on the core affects every connected system. Escalation with correct Priority 1 and a major incident declaration is the correct response.
